title: Review: shielded tx builder + BIP-39 seed fix
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — P1 bug (wrong error variant for Orchard-only UA) doesn't break payments but misleads users with 'invalid' when the address is valid. Two P2 forward-looking risks: fee formula coupling to Orchard, and overflow inconsistency that is currently impossible with real ZEC supply but would be a trap when adding multi-chain support.
Confidence: 15% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) builder.build() and FeeRule::standard() interaction not fully traced — verified algebraically that fee is the same with 1 vs 2 outputs for all n, but this is a 3-party invariant between fees.rs, tx_builder.rs, and zcash_primitives; (2) try_load_wallet_fvks passphrase prompt UX not runtime-tested (no_passphrase=false path only tested via the test that skips it). Would raise confidence if: the no-change tx test (tx_exact_amount_no_change_output) runs with real params and the fee matches FeeRule::standard() in the builder output, and if an integration test covers the passphrase prompt path.
